---
title: "Onboarding practice — participant-alias"
synthetic: true
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Synthetic practice only

Use an assigned fictional alias or public GitHub username. Do not include private or legal names unless separately approved; email addresses; telephone numbers; employer information; demographic, medical, or accessibility information; real project or partner information; private links; credentials; or sensitive narratives.
:::

## Practice identity

**Alias:** participant-alias

**Fictional role:** Documentation practice contributor

## Exercise completed

I completed the synthetic Issue-to-pull-request onboarding exercise.

## Checklist

- [ ] Located the assigned Issue
- [ ] Read the objective and acceptance criteria
- [ ] Claimed the Issue and posted a starting update
- [ ] Created an Issue-linked branch
- [ ] Opened the correct branch in GitHub Desktop
- [ ] Reviewed the complete diff
- [ ] Created a focused commit
- [ ] Pushed the branch
- [ ] Opened a linked pull request against `main`
- [ ] Requested steward review
- [ ] Responded to simulated review feedback

## Optional reflection

The workflow step that was most confusing was: [one sentence about workflow friction, or remove this sentence].

A merged submission demonstrates completion of this repository-practice exercise only. It does not establish leadership acceptance, Issue acceptance beyond the practice Issue, artifact acceptance, or broader program completion.
